primary_payload_or_object_names: Typed Content Contract;Ingress Contract Variants;Log Level Envelope
boundary_role: Client-side contract object associated with Log Level API and responsible for producing/preparing Log Level Envelope
contract_template_role: Typed/template ingress preparation; validating/hot-path split; static contract assembly; no runtime COM-interface assumption
must_provide: typed template admission compatibility;validator composition;metadata composition;timestamp stabilization composition;envelope realization routine;contract-family distinction
must_not_do: replace Assembler Ingress Port;own Queue Container slots after submission;bypass Metadata Injector;bypass Timestamp Stabilizer;act as registry delivery edge
notes: Envelope Assembler is the composed client-side ingress preparation object.

primary_payload_or_object_names: Log Level Envelope;Queue Container;Slot;Ingress Contract Variants
boundary_role: Assembler-side receiving edge for already assembled Log Level Envelope and admission into suitable Queue Container Slot
contract_template_role: Typed/template server-side receiving contract; slot targeting; queue-container admission discipline
must_provide: envelope receiving surface;contract-shape verification;queue-container targeting compatibility;slot admission capability;handoff into runtime queue structures
must_not_do: assemble envelope;replace Envelope Assembler;accept policy bundles;accept operational directives;act as registry delivery boundary;introduce container above Queue Container
notes: Assembler Ingress Port separates client-side envelope preparation from server-side runtime admission.

boundary_role: Inbound administrative/control boundary that prevents policy, metadata, and operational payloads from entering through consuming ingress
contract_template_role: Typed/template administrative contract surface for ecosystem control payloads
must_provide: policy bundle receiving surface;metadata directive receiving surface;operational directive receiving surface;ecosystem control/config receiving surface;administrative boundary separation
must_not_do: receive normal log content payloads;bypass PolicyAssigner;bypass Metadata Injector;bypass Queue Bundle Agent;collapse administrative input into consuming ingress;act as registry delivery output
notes: Administrative / Ecosystem Port is the dedicated admin/control ingress surface.

primary_payload_or_object_names: Log Level Envelope;Readiness Signal;Receiving Admission Access;Lifecycle Transfer State;Dispatcher Boundary Edge
boundary_role: Outbound registry-facing contract surface used by Dispatcher; preserves external registry abstraction and lifecycle transfer boundary
contract_template_role: Typed/template outbound registry contract; readiness/capacity gating; receiver admission; lifecycle transfer
must_provide: registry readiness contract surface;envelope handoff contract surface;receiving-side admission access;lifecycle transfer boundary discipline;boundary-sufficient registry access
must_not_do: replace Dispatcher as actor;own registry internals;own registry-side lifecycle;accept consuming content ingress;accept policy bundles;collapse into direct registry implementation access
notes: Registry / Delivery Port is the contract surface; Dispatcher remains final assembler-side actor.

boundary_role: External content-facing counterpart to Envelope Assembler and Assembler Ingress Port
contract_template_role: Typed/template content admission counterpart; validating vs hot-path contract origin
must_provide: provide typed content through correct template;preserve validating/hot-path contract variant selection
must_not_do: send policy bundles;send operational directives;replace Envelope Assembler;replace Assembler Ingress Port
notes: Level API is external to Assembler but participates in the consuming contract surface.

boundary_role: External administrative actor/source for governance and control inputs
must_provide: provide governance inputs through administrative port;respect surface separation
must_not_do: bypass Administrative / Ecosystem Port;enter through consuming ingress
notes: Assemblers Administrator is a source/counterparty, not an internal delegated entity.

contract_template_role: Policy provisioning counterpart; not assignment executor
must_provide: provide policy bundle;preserve policy object identity
must_not_do: assign directly to components;bypass PolicyAssigner;act as component mechanism host
notes: Policy Registry / Policy Source feeds policy material into Administrative / Ecosystem Port.

contract_template_role: Metadata directive counterpart; schema/directive-governed, not policy-hosted
must_provide: provide metadata directives through administrative path;preserve metadata authority
must_not_do: bypass Metadata Injector;enter through consuming ingress;become Policy Object
notes: Metadata Directive Source governs metadata input but does not create policy-hosting semantics.

boundary_role: External operational directive source for pause/drain/scale/activate style control
contract_template_role: Operational control counterpart; must be realized by queue runtime components
must_provide: provide operational directives through administrative path;affect queue eligibility/progression/state
must_not_do: mutate queue runtime directly;bypass Queue Bundle Agent;enter as log content
notes: Operational directives are admin/control inputs and are realized internally.

primary_payload_or_object_names: Readiness Signal;Receiving Admission Access;Lifecycle Transfer State;Log Level Envelope
boundary_role: External receiver after valid dispatcher-registry handoff
contract_template_role: Registry-side admission and lifecycle receiving counterpart
must_provide: provide readiness/capacity signal;provide receiving admission access;own lifecycle after valid handoff
must_not_do: be treated as assembler internal store;be bypassed by non-dispatcher handoff;leave lifecycle authority inside Assembler
notes: Log Level Records Registry is external to Assembler and receives lifecycle authority after valid handoff.

boundary_role: Explicit excluded surface outside Assembler write-side authority
contract_template_role: Exclusion contract; declares what Assembler does not own
must_provide: remain outside Assembler authority;preserve read/query exclusion
must_not_do: become Assembler delegated entity;collapse with registry storage or dispatch authority
notes: Reading / Querying Subsystem is listed here because exclusion boundaries are contract surfaces too.
